articles.title: North Atlantic Right Whales Now Have a GoFundMe
articles.description: A Cape Cod research group is turning to public donations to help fund efforts to protect one of the world's most endangered whale species.

articles.title: Humpback Whale 'Timmy' Struggles in Shallow Waters Off Germany
articles.description: A young humpback whale named Timmy by rescuers was struggling to find its way out of shallow bays off the Baltic coast of Germany after a week-long ordeal.

articles.title: New York's First Dead Whale of 2026 Washes Up on Rockaway Beach
articles.description: A sei whale, approximately 30 to 40 feet in length, was reported floating in the water and eventually washed up on 95th Street beach in the Rockaways.

articles.title: Stranded Humpback Whale Beats the Odds and Swims Out to Sea
articles.description: Against the odds, a 40-foot humpback whale stranded in shallow waters off the coast of northern Germany swam to the open sea overnight after extensive rescue efforts.

articles.title: Whale Stranded Off Germany Swims to Freedom After Days of Rescue Efforts
articles.description: A humpback whale stranded on Germany's Baltic Sea coast since early this week has freed itself and swum into deeper waters, rescuers confirmed.

articles.title: Whale Swims for Freedom After Big German Rescue Effort on Baltic Coast
articles.description: Two diggers were deployed to dredge a channel for the whale to swim into deeper water, and rescue teams worked late into the day under floodlights.

articles.title: Rescuers Work to Save a 40-Foot Humpback Whale Trapped in the Baltic Sea
articles.description: "The whale can survive around five to six days in this state if he's healthy enough," said Carsten Mannheimer of Sea Shepherd marine conservation.

articles.title: Stranded Whale Rescue Running Out of Time as Humpback Remains Stuck
articles.description: Rescuers said "every minute counts" as they battle to release the animal stuck in shallow water off the north German coast.

articles.title: Trump's Defense Secretary Targets America's Most Endangered Whales
articles.description: Hegseth seeks to approve actions that could lead to extinction of Rice's whale and killing of endangered sea turtles and sperm whales by overriding ship speed requirements.

articles.title: How Whale Poop Quietly Helps Regulate Earth's Climate and Support Ocean Life
articles.description: Whale conservation is essential for maintaining ocean productivity and climate processes—their role in nutrient cycling and carbon capture reveals the interdependence of all life on Earth.

articles.title: Officials Sound Alarm After Finding Whales and Dolphins Washed Up on UK Shores
articles.description: At least 300,000 small whales, dolphins, and porpoises are killed every year from fishing-net entanglement, prompting new rescue volunteer efforts in the UK.

articles.title: Volunteers Will Be Out to Help You Spot Gray Whales on the Oregon Coast
articles.description: Spring Whale Watch Week on the Oregon coast coincides with Spring Break, offering guided whale watching opportunities during peak gray whale migration.

articles.title: Divers Stumble Upon Elusive Giant Creatures Beneath Caribbean Waters
articles.description: Dominica established the world's first sperm whale reserve in 2025, creating a sanctuary to protect these majestic creatures from ship collisions and human-induced threats.

articles.title: Three Blue Whales Spotted Off New England Coast
articles.description: Blue whales were spotted off the Rhode Island coast by the New England Aquarium's Anderson Cabot Center for Ocean Life—an unusual occurrence in these waters.

articles.title: 2026 NOAA Funding: $51M for Right Whale Conservation & Fishing Gear Research
articles.description: A 2026 U.S. funding package directs $51 million to protect endangered North Atlantic right whales through research and gear innovation while supporting the fishing industry.

articles.title: NOAA Proposes Dynamic, Technology-Driven Whale Protections for East Coast Vessels
articles.description: NOAA is considering replacing fixed-season speed limits with a system activated by acoustic sensors, aerial drones, and near-real-time monitoring technologies.

articles.title: Pacific Whale Foundation Presents 'Ocean Conservation in Action' at Rotary Club
articles.description: Pacific Whale Foundation's presentation covers their work to protect whales and dolphins through research, conservation, education and outreach programs.

articles.title: Rare Blue Whale Sightings Highlight Need for Atlantic Protections
articles.description: Blue whale sightings emphasize the incredible diversity supported by the Northeast Canyons and Seamounts Marine National Monument amid concerns over Trump's Atlantic fishing proclamation.

articles.title: Mass Pilot Whale Stranding in Indonesia Raises Questions About Ocean Health
articles.description: Villagers in central Indonesia rescued 34 short-finned pilot whales following a mass stranding on March 9, but were unable to save 21 others despite overnight efforts.

articles.title: Endangered North Atlantic Right Whale Spotting Prompts Boating Restrictions
articles.description: Three potential mothers of the endangered North Atlantic right whale were spotted off the coast of the Carolinas, triggering vessel speed restrictions along the East Coast.

articles.title: Belugas Facing Euthanasia at Shuttered Canada Theme Park May Find US Homes
articles.description: After Marineland's closure, remaining beluga whales may be relocated to US facilities rather than face euthanasia or sale to China.

articles.title: NOAA Fisheries Considers Changing Right Whale Protections
articles.description: As more than 20 North Atlantic right whale mother and calf pairs prepare to migrate up the US Atlantic Coast, the Trump administration is considering rolling back protections.

articles.title: NOAA Considering Changes to Ship Speed Rules to Protect Endangered Whales
articles.description: NOAA seeks information about ways to reduce regulatory burdens while ensuring responsible conservation practices for North Atlantic right whales.

articles.title: Whales Are Recovering From Whaling—Can They Keep Up With Climate Change?
articles.description: In some regions, whales are returning to the oceans. Researchers track their movements with satellite tags and acoustic monitoring as populations rebuild.

articles.title: Rare Blue Whale with Albinism Spotted in Mexico in 'Unprecedented Milestone'
articles.description: A rare blue whale with albinism was spotted off the coast of Mexico. The National Commission for Natural Protected Areas confirmed this unprecedented discovery.

articles.title: Pilot Whale Strands Along Southwest Florida Coast
articles.description: A pilot whale stranded in the Naples area was euthanized, the Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission confirmed.

articles.title: Volunteers Needed as More Whales and Dolphins Strand Alive in UK
articles.description: More volunteers are being recruited to help rescue a growing number of whales, dolphins and porpoises washing up on UK shores.

articles.title: Scientists Spot Multiple Endangered Blue Whales in Southern New England Waters
articles.description: Observers with New England Aquarium documented blue whales in their southern New England survey area for the first time—a rare sighting outside Canadian feeding grounds.

articles.title: Trump Administration's Plan to Relax Right Whale Protections Raises Concerns
articles.description: The Trump administration announced plans to weaken regulations protecting endangered North Atlantic right whales, relying more heavily on technology to reduce vessel strikes.

articles.title: Festival of Whales Celebrates 55th Year at Dana Point Harbor
articles.description: Dana Point Harbor's annual Festival of Whales marks its 55th year with a three-day celebration of gray whale migration featuring parades, carnival activities, and whale watching.

articles.title: Are These Killer Whales Cannibals? Scientists Investigate Orca-on-Orca Predation
articles.description: Researchers found severed orca dorsal fins with jagged bite marks on a Russian beach, revealing evidence of killer whale-on-killer whale predation in the North Pacific.

articles.title: Whale Shark Swims 'Astounding' 1,200km from Madagascar to Seychelles
articles.description: First documented instance of a whale shark making the journey from Madagascar to Seychelles, amid declining sightings in Madagascar and increasing sightings in the Seychelles.

articles.title: Humpback Whale Recovery Is Changing Who Fathers the Calves
articles.description: As humpback whale populations recover from past whaling, older males are gaining a major advantage in reproduction—early in recovery, breeding groups were dominated by younger whales.

articles.title: Gray Whales Star at Festival of Whales, But Researchers Worry About Population
articles.description: The 52nd Festival of the Whales in Dana Point celebrates annual gray whale migration while researchers express concerns about population health.

articles.title: Chewed-Up Orca Fins Point to Cannibalism, May Explain Pod Social Structure
articles.description: Researchers found orca dorsal fins with bite marks on a Russian beach in 2022 and 2024, suggesting cannibalism may explain why some orca pods stay in tight family groups.

articles.title: NOAA Announces Planned Rollback of North Atlantic Right Whale Protections
articles.description: NOAA plans to roll back protections for North Atlantic right whales as part of ongoing deregulation efforts, citing economic impacts on coastal communities.

articles.title: NOAA Starts Rewriting Rules That Protect North Atlantic Right Whales
articles.description: Oceanic speed limits protecting endangered North Atlantic right whales will likely be rewritten as NOAA seeks ideas to reduce boat-whale collisions while considering economic vitality.

articles.title: Gulf Coast Whale Festival Spotlights Endangered Rice's Whale
articles.description: The 2026 Gulf Coast Whale Festival in Pensacola features talks on the critically endangered Rice's whale, with fewer than 100 remaining in the Gulf of Mexico.

articles.title: Feds Plan to Roll Back Speed Regulations That Protect Endangered Right Whales
articles.description: Federal regulators plan to replace seasonal speed restrictions with technology-based strike-avoidance measures, though right whales have more calves than in recent seasons.

articles.title: North Atlantic Right Whale Detected Off Cape Ann, Slow Zone Enacted
articles.description: A Cape Ann buoy identified a right whale by its unique call, triggering a slow zone for large ships and sharing data with scientists for conservation measures.

articles.title: Killer Whale Moms Struggle to Regain Body Fat with More Offspring
articles.description: Canadian research on northern resident killer whales shows orca mothers struggle to regain body fat when they have more living offspring, even when food is plentiful.

articles.title: Whale Entanglements in Fishing Gear Surge Off West Coast During Heat Waves
articles.description: The West Coast Large Whale Entanglement Response Program reports surging entanglements during marine heat waves as cold-water habitat shrinks and whales move closer to fishing gear.
